caveats: Numbers are short mirrored selfplay or spot diagnostics, not absolute poker strength.
deltaBb100 is candidate policy minus baseline policy under the listed bench conditions.
Older entries are backfilled from development-session bench output; future entries should be appended from bench.js --json runs.

entries.title: Combo-quality continuation pressure
entries.hypothesis: Facing a street reraise, weak pair / weak combo continues should fold more often while strong combo continues remain aggressive.

spotChecks.spot: 6max HU turn J8s weak pair facing 3bet
spotChecks.note: Moved obvious bad 3bet-call behavior toward fold-heavy frequency.

entries.conclusion: Large targeted win in short selfplay and fixed the observed weak-pair 3bet-call leak.

entries.title: Hand-quality pot-control tuning
entries.hypothesis: Medium made hands should not convert raw equity into large turn/river bets as often, especially in 6max non-HU pots.

effects.source: bench.js challengers; not adopted for full-ring because current fullRing override keeps 9max mostly separate

rejectedCandidates.name: range-quality-table
rejectedCandidates.reason: The broad opponent call-range read was theoretically plausible but unstable in short selfplay; 411-413 seeds were negative across 2/6/9.

rejectedCandidates.name: hand-quality-table-soft
rejectedCandidates.reason: Improved 6max in one run but hurt pure HU, so the adopted change was narrowed to 6max non-HU.

spotChecks.note: Confirmed pot-control behavior for the target leak.
Flagged as next leak: strong draws are now a bit too passive in some multiway spots.

entries.conclusion: Small adopted EV gain, mainly a leak patch. Next likely improvement is to restore pressure with high-quality draws and blockers.

entries.title: Heads-up draw-quality semi-bluff tuning
entries.hypothesis: Strong nut and combo draws should be treated as natural semi-bluffs in HU pots, but broad multiway draw aggression over-bluffs.

rejectedCandidates.name: draw-quality-soft
rejectedCandidates.reason: Won HU in one run but lost 6max/9max when applied to all postflop contexts.

rejectedCandidates.name: draw-quality
rejectedCandidates.reason: Stronger global draw aggression increased c-bet pressure but lost selfplay EV across player counts.

rejectedCandidates.name: draw-quality-six
rejectedCandidates.reason: After fixing sixMax override leakage, sixMax-only draw boost was roughly neutral to slightly negative.

spotChecks.note: Soft draw-quality restored NFD semi-bluff pressure without changing weak-pair pot-control.
Targeted draw boost did not materially reopen the second-pair leak.

entries.conclusion: Adopt HU-only draw-quality pressure; reject global draw aggression.

entries.title: Global multiway c-bet discipline
entries.hypothesis: GTO multiway theory favors tighter continuation betting with weak air; preserve value and strong draw aggression while braking weak multiway c-bets.

rejectedCandidates.name: rfi-discipline-soft/rfi-discipline/rfi-discipline-heavy
rejectedCandidates.reason: Raise-first-in discipline matched preflop theory but produced an incoherent 2p negative / 6p positive / 9p negative pattern, so it was treated as noise or benchmark-context leakage rather than a strategic conclusion; no RFI candidate was adopted.

rejectedCandidates.name: sixMax-only multiway-cbet-heavy
rejectedCandidates.reason: Initial 6max-only narrowing was treated as an empirical stopgap, not a coherent theory; external strategy references and a 9max sanity check favored making the brake field-count driven instead of table-label driven.

entries.conclusion: Adopt weak multiway c-bet brake globally; keep the RFI/HU table-size candidates rejected.

entries.title: 6max river bluffcatch discipline
entries.hypothesis: River bluffcatchers should not defend only from pot odds/MDF; on multi-seat 6max tables, large river bets should tighten hero calls when blocker quality is poor and the opponent line is strong, while pure HU and full-ring remain unchanged until they test cleanly.

rejectedCandidates.name: river-defense and river-defense-heavy
rejectedCandidates.reason: Stronger settings were too unstable and hurt at least one table size in short selfplay.

rejectedCandidates.name: global/table-wide river-defense-soft
rejectedCandidates.reason: Soft river defense helped 6max but was noisy or negative in pure HU/full-ring ablations, so adoption was narrowed to 6max including 6max heads-up decisions.

entries.conclusion: Adopt blocker- and line-pressure-aware river bluffcatch discipline only for 6max table contexts, including active heads-up spots inside a 6max table.

entries.title: Table-aware street-reraise discipline
entries.hypothesis: Weak made hands with little redraw equity should continue less often against turn-or-flop reraises, but pure heads-up should stay unchanged until it wins there. Embedded heads-up spots in 6max/full-ring tables get the brake because ranges are tighter after a multi-seat hand collapses to heads-up.

effects.source: policy construction: pure duelHeadsUp override keeps streetReraiseDisciplineScale at 0

effects.benchmark: current vs jammer smoke after adoption
current vs jammer smoke after adoption
current vs jammer smoke after adoption

spotDiagnostics.result: fold frequency moved into the ~0.76-0.78 band in seeded spot probes, close to the loose teacher target of 0.72 and far below the old over-call pattern.

rejectedCandidates.name: global reraise-discipline-soft/normal/heavy
rejectedCandidates.reason: The global normal setting helped 6max but was weaker in pure heads-up and not clearly better in full-ring, so the final policy does not touch duelHeadsUp.

rejectedCandidates.name: embedded soft/light variants
rejectedCandidates.reason: Softer full-ring settings were acceptable in 9max but lost too much in 6max narrowing, while the table-hu split kept both 6max and 9max positive in the final three-seed check.

entries.conclusion: Adopt street-reraise discipline only for embedded heads-up spots: 0.75 in 6max, 1.0 in full-ring, and 0.0 in pure heads-up.
